message: Jake
Yuriko
Who on earth told them to investigate Kristy and me?
S-sorry, I’m late!
Hey, good morning!
I saw a pair of detectives that looked like they were just down there, but was there something happening around here?
I wonder if something happened?
Oh, those must be the detectives who came to see me.
They came to see the Boss, you say!?
Boss, what on earth did you do?
It seems someone told them to investigate me regarding Eva.
Regarding Eva?
Has there been any progress since then?
That's rare,
there are things I don't know too.
Huh? W-What happened!?
Eva's dismembered body was found.
No way! Oh no, I had no idea about that at all.
They talked about it on TV this morning.
Is that so?
Oh no, I missed it.
For now, I did some first-aid stuff, but how did this happen?
Even if you ask me why,
I don't really know.
Boss,
anyway, it’s a relief that the injuries weren't too deep.
Yeah.
Welcome back, Boss. Cindy called.
Oh, what did she say?
She said it's because of a promise.
She’s waiting at Tim's place.
Got it, okay.
Huh, what's that? Doesn't the Boss look a bit refreshed?
Really? You think so?
Did something good happen?
Hmm.
Yeah, maybe it's because I arranged for Meryl to meet Cindy.
Oh.
Ah, that's great! It's finally over, huh.
Welcome back, Boss.
Look
Talk
Listen
Think
Move
System
Surroundings
About Eva
About Meryl
About Kristy
About Cindy
About Guy
About Eva
About Meryl

Exit the game
Without the two bulky cops taking up space, this room doesn't feel so cramped anymore.
If those detectives really start suspecting me, this familiar view might be my last.
Once I get my reward from Meryl, I should buy a painting. It might make this stark room a bit better.
Even though from his always cheerful expression, he looks like just a high school student.
It's impressive that you're so capable.
Maybe I should give a present once in a while to keep on your good side.
If they quit, I won’t be able to work from tomorrow.
You're always so full of energy.
I feel like I’ve become the old one here.
East Asians are super popular in New York. There must be quite a few guys hitting on them.
Why haven’t I heard any rumors at all?
When I get a message from Cindy, it's definitely about her meeting with Meryl. You should hurry to see Cindy.
Boss, the wound isn't deep, but please be careful. It's an easy place for the cut to reopen.
Yeah, thanks.
But I really didn't think it would turn out this way.
Boss, don't let it get you down.
It's the first time ending a job with a human, huh?
That's true. The best I had before was looking for pets.
I have a feeling that a glorious detective life is about to begin.
Boss, let's keep doing our best.
There are plenty of bad things, but there seem to be good ones too. Boss, you look happy right now.
After all, Cindy said she would meet her mother.
Hehe, that's great to hear.
That's wonderful news, Boss. I managed to keep my job too.
I’m flabbergasted. To think there are people who see me as the culprit.
Who was it that said such a thing?
I think it's Andy.
Andy? Wasn’t he a colleague of Eva's?
It must be rough for you, Boss. You can't really say being a detective isn't a bit shady.
Eva.
In the end, she was killed, huh?
Yeah, it seems so. But,
it might be the first time I haven't heard news about her.
That's right.
Somehow,
I'm in a situation where I can't suppress this curiosity bubbling up from within.
Ah, what kind of woman am I?
Eva was killed just when she was about to write an article about me, so Andy says I'm the culprit.
Such
I can't say I don't understand how that person feels.
But, Boss isn't the one, right?
Of course! Why would I have to do something like that?
Y-yeah, that's right.
Hehe.
Was it an article that cast suspicion on you?
They said it was a love story between Cindy and me.
Eh!? Was that really the case? Boss, you can't be underestimated.
It's definitely a lie, right?
Oh, so it's a lie.
That's true, Cindy has Tim after all.
But, isn't it strange? If Andy knew the content of the article, wouldn't he say the Boss isn't the culprit?
Or was it something worth thinking that way?
So, are you suspecting that I'm the culprit?
N-no! Not at all! Oh, you silly Boss.
Uh.
So, who on earth is the culprit?
It's a terrible incident, isn't it?
It's probably the work of a pervert.
I thought so.
But this method feels like it has an intellectual's signature.
I can sense something like an intellectual's penchant in this.
Uh.
Hmm, it seems like things have gotten pretty complicated.
Well, that's fine.
There's no point in me worrying about it.
Exactly! The Boss is more suited for finding people!
Such bloody incidents don't suit the Boss! Plus, the serious look on their face when troubled!
That's great! Now we've completed the request!
I'm really relieved that the burden has finally been lifted.
Ahh, it was a long journey to get here.
Well, now that work is over, let's dive into our hobby of detective work.
Hobby detective work?
There have been serial murder cases and various incidents happening around us, right? I'm going to investigate them on my own.
Ooh.
Well, that sounds nice too.
Ah.
It feels kind of nice.
So, have you heard from Meryl since then?
No.
I don't think you got a call, did you?
I see. Cindy agreed to meet her mother. Meryl seemed quite anxious, so let her know quickly.
Why not inform her quickly? I'm sure Meryl will be happy.
Boss, please report to Meryl soon.
We're waiting for Cindy’s contact regarding Meryl.
Even if we say it's until Tim's audition is over, that's still just an excuse, isn’t it?
I really hope something gets sorted out before Meryl gets angry.
Kristy, do you hold any grudges against the boss?
Why would you think that?
Because normally, you wouldn't think such things.
Right?
But really, what happened?
Maybe it's not just Guy, but there’s something eating away at her heart.
Boss!
Wouldn't it be better to talk to Gunter?
What'll happen to Kristy?
I haven't heard anything from Kristy, but she did say she’s okay, didn’t she, Boss?
Yeah.
But, I wonder if she really is okay?
If someone who met her says she's fine, I’m starting to believe it could be true.
That's great to hear!
Kristy, just go back to normal.
I've reported to Cindy, now it's just a matter of waiting for the day.
At noon on the day,
I'll also go to the entrance of the Guggenheim Museum,
make sure to introduce the two properly.
Haa.
I can't wait to see their surprised faces in person.
I'm getting butterflies in my stomach like it’s my own situation.
Please contact Cindy with the date and time of the mother-daughter meeting.
Cindy finally agreed to meet her mom.
That's wonderful! This means Meryl's request is perfectly handled.
Not yet. The request isn't over until the meeting is finished.
That’s true, but it’s almost here. It’s practically done, right? I'm so happy, so happy, so happy!
I hope it goes well. Maybe Meryl will give us a special bonus!
Have you heard from Cindy?
No.
Oh, right, if she doesn't contact us, Meryl's request won't progress.
That’s troubling.
It feels like we’re crawling just one step away from the goal.
About Guy, I think the baseball team is involved.
What’s wrong, suddenly asking? Is it your detective instincts?
Ooh.
Well, I actually asked someone from the baseball team persistently about Guy.
Oh really?
The Boss is pretty good at this.
Then they got all flustered and ended up telling me to never come back.
Doesn’t that mean you just scared off an annoying guy?
That’s just it, they definitely had something to hide.
No way.
Absolutely, it felt like there was some kind of secret.
Hoo.
Yeah, that's definitely the detective's intuition.
I feel like something's off.
So the Boss was also a bit of a detective, huh? I respect that.
Andy must have known the content of the article that exposed my past.
That's why he got the wrong idea that I was the culprit.
Wait, so he hasn't heard anything about Eva being involved in the Serial Murder Case?
If that’s the case…
Why did Eva say such things to me?
No way, does she think someone I know is the murderer? No way, that can't be true.
Eva was murdered. And she was dismembered.
Let's put the matter of Eva on hold for now. Another development might emerge later.
I'm glad Meryl's request is wrapped up. That's one decent job handled.
I wonder how Meryl and Cindy will react when they realize they're related?
The mother-daughter meeting might not bring about good results for the two. Meryl must be aware of that.
How could someone take such a dangerous risk? It’s beyond my understanding.
What on earth happened to Kristy?
Did the detectives say something terrible to her that made her act like that?
Even if she’s mentally unstable, losing it that much is…
After all, what Günter said might be true.
That anxiety crosses my mind.
I heard detectives went to see Kristy. I hope she's okay.
Is it really okay to let those two meet?
Maybe it’d be happier if things stayed like this for a lifetime.
Either way, my job ends here. I can only leave everything to Cindy and Meryl now.
Once this job is done, I won't see Cindy again, huh? I’m a bit anxious about what’s going to happen.
Maybe that’s just unnecessary worry.
I need to let Cindy know the date and time.
I can’t believe that stubborn kid agreed to meet her mom. Whatever the intention, maybe she's opened up a little?
I hope Cindy is actually willing to meet.
I really hope Cindy is okay with meeting her mom soon.
Garrison is definitely hiding something. Worst case scenario, Guy’s disappearance might’ve been set up by the baseball team.
Guy might be in a state of confinement.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
